senses.definition: To advise again or to provide new advice after reconsidering a situation.
examples: The consultant decided to readvise the client after new data emerged.
We will readvise the plan to better fit the budget.

senses.definition: Having been advised again or after reconsideration.
examples: The readvised proposal was submitted last week.
They readvised the schedule following the stakeholder meeting.

senses.definition: Third person singular present of readvise; advises again or provides new guidance.
examples: The analyst readvises the client after new findings.
She readvises the project plan to reflect risk changes.

senses.definition: The act of giving new advice after reconsideration.
examples: They are readvising the terms of the contract.
Readvising the timetable helped avoid clashes.

senses.definition: To advocate again or to renew advocacy for something.
examples: They readvocate the policy during the meeting.
Activists readvocate the cause after the setback.

senses.definition: Having been advocated again or having renewed advocacy.
examples: A readvocated stance emerged after the vote.
The proposal was readvocated by several supporters.

senses.definition: Actively advocating again or renewing advocacy for something.
examples: She is readvocating the reform publicly.
They are readvocating the initiative across campuses.

senses.definition: The act or instance of advocating again; renewed advocacy.
examples: The readvocation of the plan surprised some critics.
A readvocation effort began after the incident.

senses.definition: Fully prepared or available to use now.
examples: The meal is ready.
Are you ready to go?

senses.definition: State of being prepared; preparedness.
examples: They showed readiness for the challenge.
Readiness improves execution.

senses.definition: Completely; exactly; with precision (informal).
examples: He was ready not a moment late.
That was ready-made for the job.

senses.definition: The act of making something ready; preparation.
examples: She is readying the laboratory for the experiment.
They are readying for the exam period.

senses.definition: Made to be used as it is; not specially created for a purpose.
examples: They bought a readymade coat from the store.
Readymade furniture is convenient.

senses.definition: Something that is ready-made; a pre-made item.
examples: The catalog lists many readymades for quick purchase.
Artists sometimes critique readymades in museums.

senses.definition: Multiple items that are ready-made; pre-made objects.
examples: The exhibit featured readymades from various manufacturers.
Collectors liked the quirky readymades.

senses.definition: A misspelled or variant form of 're-edified' meaning to edify again; to uplift morally.
examples: The group reaedified the audience with hopeful messages.
They reaedified the curriculum to include ethics.

senses.definition: Third person singular present of reaedify; edifies again.
examples: The sermon reaedifies the crowd every week.
They reaedifies the readers with new teachings.

senses.definition: To edify again; to instruct or improve morally or intellectually again.
examples: They seek to reaedify the community after the crisis.
The book aims to reaedify readers about resilience.

senses.definition: Variant or nonce form of reaedify; to edify again.
examples: The slogan reaedifye the audience.
They tried to reaedifye beliefs about the project.

senses.definition: Past tense of reaedify; edified again.
examples: The message reaedifyed the group.
They reaedifyed their values after reflection.

senses.definition: Uncommon plural form; refers to multiple instances of reaedify in some usages.
examples: The book mentions several reaedifyes.
They discussed reaedifyes in the workshop.

senses.definition: Gerund form of reaedify; the act of edifying again.
examples: reaedifying the community takes time.
They spent hours reaedifying the audience.

senses.definition: The act of introducing air or gas into something; aeration again.
examples: The pond underwent reaeration to restore oxygen levels.
Engineers discussed reaeration of the wastewater.

senses.definition: To affect again; to produce a new emotional or psychological effect.
examples: The news reaffects the audience deeply.
Their words reaffect the mood of the room.

senses.definition: Renewed feeling of affection; rekindling of love or liking.
examples: A reaffection grew between the partners.
Her reaffection surprised him.

senses.definition: To affiliate again; to rejoin a group or organization.
examples: The club decided to reaffiliate with the national federation.
They chose to reaffiliate after a brief separation.

senses.definition: Past tense of reaffiliate; joined again to an organization.
examples: They reaffiliated with the federation last year.
The school reaffiliated after governance changes.

senses.definition: Gerund form of reaffiliate; the act of joining again.
examples: The group is reaffiliating with the national body.
They are reaffiliating to strengthen ties.
